items.title: Gemini gets a native Mac app with screen-aware desktop help
items.summary: Google launched a native Gemini app for macOS 15 and up, adding an Option-Space shortcut, window sharing, and quick access to image and video generation from the desktop.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Gemini is moving from a browser destination into a resident desktop assistant that can use local screen context without forcing users out of their workflow.

items.title: OpenAI pushes for a bigger AI role in life sciences
items.summary: OpenAI is arguing that AI can accelerate biomedical research by connecting fragmented knowledge, designing tools, automating labs, and shortening experiment cycles.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: OpenAI is tying frontier model progress to regulated, high-value scientific workflows where adoption depends as much on policy and trust as raw capability.

items.title: Claude outage hits Claude.ai, API, and Claude Code users
items.summary: Anthropic confirmed elevated errors affecting Claude.ai, the API, and Claude Code, with users reporting login failures and interrupted prompt handling during the April 15 incident.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: recurring downtime is now a product signal for AI tools that users rely on for daily coding and operational workflows.

items.title: OpenAI updates Agents SDK with sandbox and harness support
items.summary: OpenAI updated its Agents SDK with sandboxing and in-distribution harness features so enterprises can build safer long-horizon agents on frontier models.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: enterprise agent adoption is moving from demos toward controlled execution environments, approved tools, and deployable safety boundaries.

items.title: Hightouch hits $100M ARR on AI-powered marketing tools
items.summary: Hightouch says its AI marketing product helped drive the company to $100 million in ARR by letting brands generate personalized campaigns from their own creative systems.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: enterprise buyers are paying for AI systems that stay grounded in brand assets and customer data instead of generic image generation.

items.title: AI learning app Gizmo raises $22M after reaching 13M users
items.summary: Gizmo, an AI learning app focused on study workflows, raised $22 million after growing to 13 million users.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: education remains one of the clearest consumer AI wedges when products tie generation to memory, practice, and repeat use.

items.title: Emergent moves into vibe-coding AI agents
items.summary: India-based Emergent is expanding from vibe coding into an OpenClaw-like AI agent space for building and operating software workflows.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: agentic coding competition is widening beyond the best-known US developer tools into regional products with local distribution advantages.

items.title: Adobe Firefly AI Assistant starts orchestrating Creative Cloud tasks
items.summary: Adobe launched Firefly AI Assistant in public beta, giving users an agent that can work across Firefly, Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Express, Illustrator, and related apps.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: creative AI is shifting from single-output generation toward assistants that can operate across full production workflows.

items.title: Gitar emerges with $9M for agentic code security
items.summary: Gitar emerged from stealth with $9 million for an agent-based platform aimed at helping teams secure code.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: AI coding tools are creating demand for automated review and remediation systems that can keep up with faster software generation.

items.title: Parasail raises $32M for tokenmaxxing AI developers
items.summary: Parasail raised $32 million for infrastructure aimed at developers who need to spend and manage large token volumes for AI products.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: AI infrastructure demand is broadening from raw GPUs to billing, routing, and usage layers that make high-token applications practical.

items.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS adds expressive speech controls
items.summary: Google introduced Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS with improved speech quality, audio tags for style and pacing control, 70-plus language support, and SynthID watermarking.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: text-to-speech models are becoming programmable creative systems, not just narration utilities, with developer controls exposed through AI Studio and Vertex AI.

items.title: GitHub lets enterprises enable Copilot cloud agent by organization
items.summary: GitHub added controls for selectively enabling Copilot cloud agent access for specific organizations, including new API endpoints and AI Controls page settings.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: enterprise AI coding agents are shifting from all-or-nothing rollout decisions toward staged adoption with policy controls.

items.title: GitHub adds model selection for Claude and Codex agents
items.summary: GitHub now lets users choose models when starting Claude and Codex third-party coding agent tasks on github.com, including Claude Sonnet and Opus options plus GPT-5.2-Codex, GPT-5.3-Codex, and GPT-5.4.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: agent platforms are turning model choice into a first-class workflow control instead of hiding it behind a single assistant brand.
